label: AWS RDS Postgres
description: Connect to an AWS RDS PostgreSQL instance using your RDS endpoint, credentials, and optional SSL.

form.title: Connect AWS RDS Postgres
form.subtitle: Enter your RDS endpoint and database credentials to connect Anton to your PostgreSQL instance.

methods.label: Host + Credentials
methods.description: Paste your RDS endpoint, port, database name, and credentials. The simplest and most direct way to connect to an RDS PostgreSQL instance.
methods.how_to:
## Connect via RDS Endpoint & Credentials

### Find your RDS endpoint

1. Open the [AWS Console](https://console.aws.amazon.com/) and sign in.
2. Navigate to **Services → RDS** (or search for `RDS` in the top bar).
3. In the left sidebar click **Databases**.
4. Click the name of your PostgreSQL instance.
5. On the instance detail page, scroll to the **Connectivity & security** tab.
6. Copy the **Endpoint** (looks like `mydb.xxxxxxxxxx.us-east-1.rds.amazonaws.com`). The **Port** is shown just below — default is `5432`.

### Security group / VPC access

- Your RDS instance must be **publicly accessible** OR Anton must run inside the same VPC.
- In the **Connectivity & security** tab, click the linked **VPC security group** and confirm there is an inbound rule allowing TCP on port `5432` from your IP (or `0.0.0.0/0` for unrestricted — not recommended for production).

### SSL

- AWS RDS enforces SSL by default on most instance classes. Enable the **Require SSL** toggle below to tell Anton to use `sslmode=require`.
- Download the [AWS RDS root CA bundle](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonRDS/latest/UserGuide/UsingWithRDS.SSL.html) if you need certificate verification.

### Notes

- The master user is set at instance creation. If you've lost it, reset it via **RDS → Databases → Modify → Master password**.
- For production, prefer a dedicated read-only database user with scoped permissions instead of the master user.

fields.name: host
fields.label: RDS Endpoint
fields.description: Found in AWS Console → RDS → Databases → [your instance] → Connectivity & security tab.

fields.name: port
fields.label: Port
fields.description: Default PostgreSQL port is 5432. Change only if your instance uses a custom port.

fields.name: database
fields.label: Database Name
fields.description: The name of the PostgreSQL database you want to connect to.

fields.name: user
fields.label: Username
fields.description: Database user — typically the master user or a dedicated app user with appropriate permissions.

fields.name: password
fields.label: Password
fields.description: Password for the database user above. Stored encrypted locally.

fields.name: ssl_enabled
fields.label: Require SSL
fields.description: Enable SSL (sslmode=require). Recommended — AWS RDS enforces SSL on most instance types by default.

methods.label: IAM Database Authentication
methods.description: Use AWS IAM credentials to generate a short-lived auth token instead of a static password. Requires IAM DB authentication enabled on the RDS instance.
methods.how_to:
## IAM Database Authentication

Instead of a static password, AWS generates a short-lived token (valid 15 minutes) signed with your IAM credentials.

### Prerequisites

1. In the AWS Console go to **RDS → Databases → [your instance] → Modify**.
2. Under **Database options**, enable **IAM database authentication**. Apply immediately.
3. In **IAM → Policies**, create a policy that allows `rds-db:connect` for your DB resource ARN:
   - Resource ARN format: `arn:aws:rds-db:<region>:<account-id>:dbuser:<db-instance-resource-id>/<db-username>`
4. Attach the policy to the IAM user or role Anton will use.
5. In your PostgreSQL database, create the user and grant the `rds_iam` role:
   - `CREATE USER myiamuser; GRANT rds_iam TO myiamuser;`

### Finding your values

- **Region**: visible in the AWS Console top-right corner (e.g. `us-east-1`).
- **DB Resource ID**: on the **Configuration** tab of your RDS instance (e.g. `db-ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOP`).
- **Access Key / Secret**: from **IAM → Users → [user] → Security credentials → Create access key**.

### Notes

- SSL is mandatory for IAM auth — the toggle is always on.
- The access key is used only to sign a token request; the actual DB connection still uses the token.

fields.name: host
fields.label: RDS Endpoint
fields.description: Found in AWS Console → RDS → Databases → [your instance] → Connectivity & security tab.

fields.name: database
fields.label: Database Name
fields.description: The name of the PostgreSQL database you want to connect to.

fields.name: db_username
fields.label: Database Username
fields.description: The PostgreSQL user that has been granted the rds_iam role.

fields.name: aws_region
fields.label: AWS Region
fields.description: The AWS region where your RDS instance is running (e.g. us-east-1, eu-west-2).

fields.name: aws_access_key_id
fields.label: AWS Access Key ID
fields.description: IAM user access key ID with rds-db:connect permission. From IAM → Users → Security credentials.

fields.name: aws_secret_access_key
fields.label: AWS Secret Access Key
fields.description: Secret paired with the Access Key ID above. Stored encrypted locally.
